  "instructions":"Do not open this question paper until you are told to do so. Write your name and index numbers as they appear on your registration form. This paper consists of three sections A, B and C. SECTION A: This section is compulsory (55 marks). SECTION B: Attempt any THREE questions. (30 marks). SECTION C: Attempt only one question. (15 marks). Use only blue or black pen and pencil.",
   "answerKey":{
      "1":"a) Wind speed - kilometres per hour (knots) b) Amount of cloud - okta c) Air pressure - millibars",
      "2":"Vertical aerial photographs, oblique aerial photographs",
      "3":"Both study the location of places using latitudes and longitudes, compass directions, bearings and calculations of gradients. Both deal with statistics, both can be used to calculate the speed of comets, asteroids, etc",
      "4":"The sun and the moon",
      "5":"Eclipse of the moon and eclipse of the sun",
      "6":"Rotation of the earth",
      "7":"a) A year with 366 days. b) Addition of a % rotation of each year after every 4 ordinary years",
      "8":"Relief/orographic rainfall, Convectional rainfall, Frontal/eyclonic rainfall",
      "9":"30°N - Tropic of Cancer (30° parallel North), 30°S - Tropic of Capricorn (30° parallel South)",
      "10":"a) Trees have climbing plants; they are evergreen; they have 3 canopies. b) They modify climate by causing rainfall; they provide fuel in form of charcoal and firewood; they provide fertile soils for agriculture; they provide trees for building",
      "11":"A - River Confluence, B - River meander, C - Oxbow, D - Levees",
      "12":"a) Dendritic drainage pattern, b) Radial drainage pattern, c) Trellis drainage pattern",
      "13":"a) Vulcanicity is the process through which molten materials from the earth’s crust are intruded into or extruded at the surface of the earth. b) SIAL - Silica, Aluminium, SIMA - Silica, Magnesium",
      "14":"Definition Type of rock a) A rock type which has been Metamorphic changed by heat and or pressure, b) A rock type which may have Sedimentary been deposited under water, c) A rock type that was once Igneous molten",
      "15":"a) Fold structure, b) North West Africa e.g. Atlas Mountain, South Africa e.g. Cape ranges",
      "16":"a) Montane vegetation, b) Thick barks, vegetation has grasses, c) Relief, difference in altitude, uneven rainfall, uneven temperature",
      "17":"a) i) Lake Kivu was formed as a result of faulting (graben lake). ii) Lake Rweru was formed as a result of alluvial deposits (backponding of rivers), valley flooding and reversal drainage. iii) Lake Ruhondo was formed as a result of vulcanicity, causing lava damming",
      "18":"a) Netherlands, b) Germany - Berlin; Netherlands - Amsterdam; Belgium-Brussels; Luxemburg - Luxemburg",
      "19":"a) Volcanic soils, b) Mountainous relief, heavy rainfall, poor soils, poor methods of farming, deforestation",
      "20":"a) Amazon rain forest, b) Brazil, Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru",
      "21":"a) Nyabarongo, Ruvubu, b) Rusumo falls",
      "22":"a) The Yangtze; The Huangte (Yellow river), b) Mount Everest - Himalayas",
      "23":"a) (i) Identify the highest and the lowest attitude of the area covered by the map, (ii) Compare the relief of Nyakabungo to that of Rurenge",
      "24":"a) (i) Name the type of transport shown on the photograph, (ii) Give reasons to support the answer given in (a) (i) above"
   },
   "allowSelectiveAnswering":true,
   "sectionBRequiredQuestions":3,
   "sectionCRequiredQuestions":1,
   "sections":[
      {
         "name":"A",
         "description":"Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":1,
               "text":"Identify units for measuring the following elements of weather: (a) Wind speed (1 mark) (b) Amount of cloud (1 mark) (c) Air Pressure (1 mark)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Wind speed - kilometres per hour (knots) b) Amount of cloud - okta c) Air pressure - millibars",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":2,
               "text":"Mention two types of aerial photographs",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Vertical aerial photographs, oblique aerial photographs",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":3,
               "text":"How is geography related to mathematics?",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Both study the location of places using latitudes and longitudes, compass directions, bearings and calculations of gradients. Both deal with statistics, both can be used to calculate the speed of comets, asteroids, etc",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":4,
               "text":"Name two brightest heavenly bodies in the sky",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"The sun and the moon",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":5,
               "text":"What are the two kinds of Eclipses?",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Eclipse of the moon and eclipse of the sun",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":6,
               "text":"What causes time difference between longitudes?",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Rotation of the earth",
               "points":1
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":7,
               "text":"(a) What is meant by a leap year? (1 mark) (b) How does a leap year come about? (2 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) A year with 366 days. b) Addition of a % rotation of each year after every 4 ordinary years",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":8,
               "text":"Describe three forms of rainfall",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Relief/orographic rainfall, Convectional rainfall, Frontal/eyclonic rainfall",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":9,
               "text":"Name tropics named 30°S and 30°N in the following map",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"30°N - Tropic of Cancer (30° parallel North), 30°S - Tropic of Capricorn (30° parallel South)",
               "points":2
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":10,
               "text":"(a) Describe the three characteristics of Birunga forests. (3 marks) (b) Explain the benefits of Birunga forest to the population living nearby. (3 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Trees have climbing plants; they are evergreen; they have 3 canopies. b) They modify climate by causing rainfall; they provide fuel in form of charcoal and firewood; they provide fertile soils for agriculture; they provide trees for building",
               "points":6
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":11,
               "text":"Identify the river features labelled as A, B, C and D",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"A - River Confluence, B - River meander, C - Oxbow, D - Levees",
               "points":4
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":12,
               "text":"Give the names of the following drainage patterns",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Dendritic drainage pattern, b) Radial drainage pattern, c) Trellis drainage pattern",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":13,
               "text":"(a) Briefly explain the process of vulcanicity. (4 marks) (b) What are the mineral constituents of SIAL and SIMA? (4 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Vulcanicity is the process through which molten materials from the earth’s crust are intruded into or extruded at the surface of the earth. b) SIAL - Silica, Aluminium, SIMA - Silica, Magnesium",
               "points":8
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":14,
               "text":"Copy the table and write the three names alongside the correct definition",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Definition Type of rock a) A rock type which has been Metamorphic changed by heat and or pressure, b) A rock type which may have Sedimentary been deposited under water, c) A rock type that was once Igneous molten",
               "points":3
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":15,
               "text":"(a) Draw a fold structure and name its three main parts. (2 marks) (b) Name two African areas where Fold Mountains are found. (2 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Fold structure, b) North West Africa e.g. Atlas Mountain, South Africa e.g. Cape ranges",
               "points":4
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":16,
               "text":"(a) Name the type of vegetation dominant in the western part of Rwanda. (1 mark) (b) What are the characteristics of the type of vegetation mentioned in (a) above. (3 marks) (c) Explain two factors responsible for the existence of the vegetation. (2 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Montane vegetation, b) Thick barks, vegetation has grasses, c) Relief, difference in altitude, uneven rainfall, uneven temperature",
               "points":6
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"B",
         "description":"Short Answer Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":17,
               "text":"(a) Explain the ways in which the lakes below were formed. (3 marks) (i) Lake Kivu (ii) Lake Rweru (iii) Lake Ruhondo (b) Explain with relevant examples the uses of different lakes in Rwanda. (7 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) i) Lake Kivu was formed as a result of faulting (graben lake). ii) Lake Rweru was formed as a result of alluvial deposits (backponding of rivers), valley flooding and reversal drainage. iii) Lake Ruhondo was formed as a result of vulcanicity, causing lava damming. b) Water transport e.g. on lake Kivu, natural boundaries e.g Lake Rweru; fishing e.g. on Lake Kivu; favour tourism e.g. tourists visit them; irrigation e.g. Lake Muhazi; sports and game like swimming on Lake Kivu; mining methane gas on Lake Kivu",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":18,
               "text":"(a) Name two countries crossed by river Rhine. (1 mark) (b) Name capital city of one of countries named in (a) above. (2 marks) (c) Explain how useful the river Rhine is to the population of countries mentioned in (a) (4 marks) (d) Explain problems associated with the existence of river Rhine. (3 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Netherlands, b) Germany - Berlin; Netherlands - Amsterdam; Belgium-Brussels; Luxemburg - Luxemburg, c) Transport, promote the industrial sector in Europe e.g. the Ruhr, employment opportunities e.g. fishing; promote urban development hence creating market in Essen, used for fishing and navigation, d) Silting of the river, accidents are common on River Rhine; it has a small volume of water hence big ships can’t sail; wild animals; floods",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":19,
               "text":"(a) Name the type of soils found in the Birunga region. (1 mark) (b) Explain 4 causes of soil erosion in the area mentioned in (a) above. (4 marks) (c) Describe different ways of keeping the soil from being eroded. (5 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Volcanic soils, b) Mountainous relief, heavy rainfall, poor soils, poor methods of farming, deforestation, c) Terracing, afforestation and re-afforestation, mulching, contour ploughing, agro forestry, reduced grazing",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":20,
               "text":"(a) Name the dominant rain forest in parts of South America. (1 mark) (b) Identify three countries that are occupied by the forest mentioned in (a) above. (3 marks) (c) Describe the characteristics of the rain forest in (a) above. (3 marks) (d) Explain factors responsible for the existence of the rain forest said in (a) above. (3 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Amazon rain forest, b) Brazil, Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, c) Evergreen, little undergrowth, thick and impenetrable, receive heavy rainfall, trees do not grow in pure strands, d) Heavy rainfall (good climate), fertile soils, large pieces of land, good government policy, latitude /temperature",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":21,
               "text":"(a) Name two rivers that meet to form river Akagera. (2 marks) (b) Name the water fall found on the river Akagera. (1 mark) (c) Explain four uses of the fall mentioned in (b) above. (4 marks) (d) Describe the importance of the Akagera swamp to the population of the area. (3 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) Nyabarongo, Ruvubu, b) Rusumo falls, c) Used for tourism, generation of HEP, used for research, used for recreational purposes e.g. swimming, water from the river is used for domestic use, d) It is a source of raw materials for handcraft; it is a source of water; it is used for scientific research; source of rainfall; medicinal purposes",
               "points":10
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":22,
               "text":"(a) Mention two largest rivers of China. (2 marks) (b) Name the highest peak in the world and the mountain range where it is found. (2 marks) (c) Mention the mountain range that separates Europe and Asia. (1 mark) (d) Explain the importance of high mountains mentioned in (a). (5 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"a) The Yangtze; The Huangte (Yellow river), b) Mount Everest - Himalayas, c) Ural mountain, d) Attracts tourists; natural boundary between Europe and Asia; cause relief rainfall; used for mining; source of rivers e.g. Ural; favours agriculture due to fertile volcanic soils",
               "points":10
            }
         ]
      },
      {
         "name":"C",
         "description":"Essay/Long Answer Questions",
         "questions":[
            {
               "questionNumber":23,
               "text":"Study the map extract of KABARONDO and answer the questions that follow: (a) (i) Identify the highest and the lowest attitude of the area covered by the map. (2 marks) (ii) Compare the relief of Nyakabungo to that of Rurenge. (2 marks) (b) (i) Identify the type of settlement in the map and give evidence to support your answer. (2 marks) (ii) Identify the man made feature shown on grid reference 4775500 (1 mark) (iii) What are two types of scale used on the map? (2 marks) (c) Name two economic activities of the area covered by the map. (2 marks) (d) (i) Mention two main functions in the Kabarondo centre. (2 marks) (ii) Measure the distance in km of the feeder road of Bitoma from the grid reference 560500 to the main murrum road of Kabarondo, (2 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Not provided",
               "points":15
            },
            {
               "questionNumber":24,
               "text":"Study the photo below and answer the questions that follow: (a) (i) Name the type of transport shown on the photograph. (1 mark) (ii) Give reasons to support the answer given in (a) (i) above. (2 marks) (iii) Describe the type of vegetation in the background. (2 marks) (b) Examine the problems facing the type of transport indicated in (a) (i) above. (5 marks) (c) Suggest possible strategies which the government of Rwanda could use to solve or minimize on the transport problems examined in (b) above. (5 marks)",
               "type":"open-ended",
               "passage":"",
               "correctAnswer":"Not provided",
               "points":15
            }
         ]
      }
   ]
}

parsedContent.instructions: Do not open this question paper until you are told to do so. Write your name and index numbers as they appear on your registration form. This paper consists of three sections A, B and C. SECTION A: This section is compulsory (55 marks). SECTION B: Attempt any THREE questions. (30 marks). SECTION C: Attempt only one question. (15 marks). Use only blue or black pen and pencil.

answerKey.1: a) Wind speed - kilometres per hour (knots) b) Amount of cloud - okta c) Air pressure - millibars
answerKey.2: Vertical aerial photographs, oblique aerial photographs
answerKey.3: Both study the location of places using latitudes and longitudes, compass directions, bearings and calculations of gradients. Both deal with statistics, both can be used to calculate the speed of comets, asteroids, etc
answerKey.5: Eclipse of the moon and eclipse of the sun
answerKey.7: a) A year with 366 days. b) Addition of a % rotation of each year after every 4 ordinary years
answerKey.8: Relief/orographic rainfall, Convectional rainfall, Frontal/eyclonic rainfall
answerKey.9: 30°N - Tropic of Cancer (30° parallel North), 30°S - Tropic of Capricorn (30° parallel South)
answerKey.10: a) Trees have climbing plants; they are evergreen; they have 3 canopies. b) They modify climate by causing rainfall; they provide fuel in form of charcoal and firewood; they provide fertile soils for agriculture; they provide trees for building
answerKey.11: A - River Confluence, B - River meander, C - Oxbow, D - Levees
answerKey.12: a) Dendritic drainage pattern, b) Radial drainage pattern, c) Trellis drainage pattern
answerKey.13: a) Vulcanicity is the process through which molten materials from the earth’s crust are intruded into or extruded at the surface of the earth. b) SIAL - Silica, Aluminium, SIMA - Silica, Magnesium
answerKey.14: Definition Type of rock a) A rock type which has been Metamorphic changed by heat and or pressure, b) A rock type which may have Sedimentary been deposited under water, c) A rock type that was once Igneous molten
answerKey.15: a) Fold structure, b) North West Africa e.g. Atlas Mountain, South Africa e.g. Cape ranges
answerKey.16: a) Montane vegetation, b) Thick barks, vegetation has grasses, c) Relief, difference in altitude, uneven rainfall, uneven temperature
answerKey.17: a) i) Lake Kivu was formed as a result of faulting (graben lake). ii) Lake Rweru was formed as a result of alluvial deposits (backponding of rivers), valley flooding and reversal drainage. iii) Lake Ruhondo was formed as a result of vulcanicity, causing lava damming
answerKey.18: a) Netherlands, b) Germany - Berlin; Netherlands - Amsterdam; Belgium-Brussels; Luxemburg - Luxemburg
answerKey.19: a) Volcanic soils, b) Mountainous relief, heavy rainfall, poor soils, poor methods of farming, deforestation
answerKey.20: a) Amazon rain forest, b) Brazil, Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru
answerKey.21: a) Nyabarongo, Ruvubu, b) Rusumo falls
answerKey.22: a) The Yangtze; The Huangte (Yellow river), b) Mount Everest - Himalayas
answerKey.23: a) (i) Identify the highest and the lowest attitude of the area covered by the map, (ii) Compare the relief of Nyakabungo to that of Rurenge
answerKey.24: a) (i) Name the type of transport shown on the photograph, (ii) Give reasons to support the answer given in (a) (i) above

sections.name: A
sections.description: Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions

questions.text: Identify units for measuring the following elements of weather: (a) Wind speed (1 mark) (b) Amount of cloud (1 mark) (c) Air Pressure (1 mark)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Wind speed - kilometres per hour (knots) b) Amount of cloud - okta c) Air pressure - millibars

questions.text: Mention two types of aerial photographs
questions.correctAnswer: Vertical aerial photographs, oblique aerial photographs

questions.text: How is geography related to mathematics?
questions.correctAnswer: Both study the location of places using latitudes and longitudes, compass directions, bearings and calculations of gradients. Both deal with statistics, both can be used to calculate the speed of comets, asteroids, etc

questions.text: Name two brightest heavenly bodies in the sky
questions.correctAnswer: The sun and the moon

questions.text: What are the two kinds of Eclipses?
questions.correctAnswer: Eclipse of the moon and eclipse of the sun

questions.text: What causes time difference between longitudes?
questions.correctAnswer: Rotation of the earth

questions.text: (a) What is meant by a leap year? (1 mark) (b) How does a leap year come about? (2 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) A year with 366 days. b) Addition of a % rotation of each year after every 4 ordinary years

questions.text: Describe three forms of rainfall
questions.correctAnswer: Relief/orographic rainfall, Convectional rainfall, Frontal/eyclonic rainfall

questions.text: Name tropics named 30°S and 30°N in the following map
questions.correctAnswer: 30°N - Tropic of Cancer (30° parallel North), 30°S - Tropic of Capricorn (30° parallel South)

questions.text: (a) Describe the three characteristics of Birunga forests. (3 marks) (b) Explain the benefits of Birunga forest to the population living nearby. (3 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Trees have climbing plants; they are evergreen; they have 3 canopies. b) They modify climate by causing rainfall; they provide fuel in form of charcoal and firewood; they provide fertile soils for agriculture; they provide trees for building

questions.text: Identify the river features labelled as A, B, C and D
questions.correctAnswer: A - River Confluence, B - River meander, C - Oxbow, D - Levees

questions.text: Give the names of the following drainage patterns
questions.correctAnswer: a) Dendritic drainage pattern, b) Radial drainage pattern, c) Trellis drainage pattern

questions.text: (a) Briefly explain the process of vulcanicity. (4 marks) (b) What are the mineral constituents of SIAL and SIMA? (4 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Vulcanicity is the process through which molten materials from the earth’s crust are intruded into or extruded at the surface of the earth. b) SIAL - Silica, Aluminium, SIMA - Silica, Magnesium

questions.text: Copy the table and write the three names alongside the correct definition
questions.correctAnswer: Definition Type of rock a) A rock type which has been Metamorphic changed by heat and or pressure, b) A rock type which may have Sedimentary been deposited under water, c) A rock type that was once Igneous molten

questions.text: (a) Draw a fold structure and name its three main parts. (2 marks) (b) Name two African areas where Fold Mountains are found. (2 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Fold structure, b) North West Africa e.g. Atlas Mountain, South Africa e.g. Cape ranges

questions.text: (a) Name the type of vegetation dominant in the western part of Rwanda. (1 mark) (b) What are the characteristics of the type of vegetation mentioned in (a) above. (3 marks) (c) Explain two factors responsible for the existence of the vegetation. (2 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Montane vegetation, b) Thick barks, vegetation has grasses, c) Relief, difference in altitude, uneven rainfall, uneven temperature

questions.text: (a) Explain the ways in which the lakes below were formed. (3 marks) (i) Lake Kivu (ii) Lake Rweru (iii) Lake Ruhondo (b) Explain with relevant examples the uses of different lakes in Rwanda. (7 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) i) Lake Kivu was formed as a result of faulting (graben lake). ii) Lake Rweru was formed as a result of alluvial deposits (backponding of rivers), valley flooding and reversal drainage. iii) Lake Ruhondo was formed as a result of vulcanicity, causing lava damming. b) Water transport e.g. on lake Kivu, natural boundaries e.g Lake Rweru; fishing e.g. on Lake Kivu; favour tourism e.g. tourists visit them; irrigation e.g. Lake Muhazi; sports and game like swimming on Lake Kivu; mining methane gas on Lake Kivu

questions.text: (a) Name two countries crossed by river Rhine. (1 mark) (b) Name capital city of one of countries named in (a) above. (2 marks) (c) Explain how useful the river Rhine is to the population of countries mentioned in (a) (4 marks) (d) Explain problems associated with the existence of river Rhine. (3 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Netherlands, b) Germany - Berlin; Netherlands - Amsterdam; Belgium-Brussels; Luxemburg - Luxemburg, c) Transport, promote the industrial sector in Europe e.g. the Ruhr, employment opportunities e.g. fishing; promote urban development hence creating market in Essen, used for fishing and navigation, d) Silting of the river, accidents are common on River Rhine; it has a small volume of water hence big ships can’t sail; wild animals; floods

questions.text: (a) Name the type of soils found in the Birunga region. (1 mark) (b) Explain 4 causes of soil erosion in the area mentioned in (a) above. (4 marks) (c) Describe different ways of keeping the soil from being eroded. (5 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Volcanic soils, b) Mountainous relief, heavy rainfall, poor soils, poor methods of farming, deforestation, c) Terracing, afforestation and re-afforestation, mulching, contour ploughing, agro forestry, reduced grazing

questions.text: (a) Name the dominant rain forest in parts of South America. (1 mark) (b) Identify three countries that are occupied by the forest mentioned in (a) above. (3 marks) (c) Describe the characteristics of the rain forest in (a) above. (3 marks) (d) Explain factors responsible for the existence of the rain forest said in (a) above. (3 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Amazon rain forest, b) Brazil, Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, c) Evergreen, little undergrowth, thick and impenetrable, receive heavy rainfall, trees do not grow in pure strands, d) Heavy rainfall (good climate), fertile soils, large pieces of land, good government policy, latitude /temperature

questions.text: (a) Name two rivers that meet to form river Akagera. (2 marks) (b) Name the water fall found on the river Akagera. (1 mark) (c) Explain four uses of the fall mentioned in (b) above. (4 marks) (d) Describe the importance of the Akagera swamp to the population of the area. (3 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) Nyabarongo, Ruvubu, b) Rusumo falls, c) Used for tourism, generation of HEP, used for research, used for recreational purposes e.g. swimming, water from the river is used for domestic use, d) It is a source of raw materials for handcraft; it is a source of water; it is used for scientific research; source of rainfall; medicinal purposes

questions.text: (a) Mention two largest rivers of China. (2 marks) (b) Name the highest peak in the world and the mountain range where it is found. (2 marks) (c) Mention the mountain range that separates Europe and Asia. (1 mark) (d) Explain the importance of high mountains mentioned in (a). (5 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: a) The Yangtze; The Huangte (Yellow river), b) Mount Everest - Himalayas, c) Ural mountain, d) Attracts tourists; natural boundary between Europe and Asia; cause relief rainfall; used for mining; source of rivers e.g. Ural; favours agriculture due to fertile volcanic soils

questions.text: Study the map extract of KABARONDO and answer the questions that follow: (a) (i) Identify the highest and the lowest attitude of the area covered by the map. (2 marks) (ii) Compare the relief of Nyakabungo to that of Rurenge. (2 marks) (b) (i) Identify the type of settlement in the map and give evidence to support your answer. (2 marks) (ii) Identify the man made feature shown on grid reference 4775500 (1 mark) (iii) What are two types of scale used on the map? (2 marks) (c) Name two economic activities of the area covered by the map. (2 marks) (d) (i) Mention two main functions in the Kabarondo centre. (2 marks) (ii) Measure the distance in km of the feeder road of Bitoma from the grid reference 560500 to the main murrum road of Kabarondo, (2 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: Not provided

questions.text: Study the photo below and answer the questions that follow: (a) (i) Name the type of transport shown on the photograph. (1 mark) (ii) Give reasons to support the answer given in (a) (i) above. (2 marks) (iii) Describe the type of vegetation in the background. (2 marks) (b) Examine the problems facing the type of transport indicated in (a) (i) above. (5 marks) (c) Suggest possible strategies which the government of Rwanda could use to solve or minimize on the transport problems examined in (b) above. (5 marks)
questions.correctAnswer: Not provided
